name: Semax
subtitle: Synthetic ACTH Analog | Nootropic & Neuroprotective Peptide
overview: Semax is a synthetic heptapeptide derived from adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH) fragment 4-10, originally developed in Russia for stroke recovery. It achieves enhanced CNS penetration through direct transport via olfactory epithelium and trigeminal nerves, bypassing the blood-brain barrier.
mechanism: Rapidly increases BDNF levels, modulates dopaminergic and serotonergic systems, and achieves direct brain delivery through olfactory transport with 0.093% blood-brain barrier penetration (vs 0.01% IV).
keyBenefits: Rapid brain delivery via intranasal route

items.name: Memory Enhancement
items.description: Improved short-term and working memory performance in fatigued individuals (71% vs 41% accuracy).

items.name: Attention and Focus
items.description: Enhanced sustained attention during demanding cognitive tasks.

items.name: Learning Acceleration
items.description: Faster acquisition of new information and improved retention.

items.name: Stroke Recovery
items.description: Accelerated rehabilitation with increased BDNF levels in stroke patients.

items.name: Brain Trauma Support
items.description: Supports recovery from traumatic brain injury.

items.name: Neurodegenerative Prevention
items.description: Shows anti-amyloid properties in Alzheimer's models.

items.name: BDNF Upregulation
items.description: Supports neurogenesis through increased brain-derived neurotrophic factor.

items.name: Neural Connectivity
items.description: Enhanced neural connections and brain network activity.

items.name: Stress Resilience
items.description: Improved ability to cope with cognitive stress.

deliveryMethods.overview: Intranasal is the primary and most effective route, providing direct brain delivery through olfactory transport. Onset 15-30 minutes, peak 1-2 hours, duration 4-6 hours.

steps: For research: reconstitute powder with sterile saline
Use preservative-free saline for nasal administration
Refrigerate and use within recommended timeframe

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection provides reliable dosing. Onset 30-60 minutes, peak 2-4 hours, duration 6-8 hours.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Inject water slowly down the side of the vial to avoid foaming
Draw into syringe using appropriate gauge needle

interactions.notes: Do NOT use simultaneously. Choose one or the other - they are variants of the same peptide.
Both support neuroplasticity through different mechanisms.
May enhance stimulant effects - monitor carefully and reduce stimulant doses.
Theoretical monoamine system risk; use caution.

timeline.effects: Initial cognitive clarity and focus enhancement, possible mild nasal sensation
Improved attention span during demanding tasks
Peak cognitive benefits with improved stress resilience and mental flexibility
Gradual baseline return over 3-7 days; some benefits persist

stopSigns: Severe nasal irritation, bleeding, or persistent congestion
Unusual anxiety, agitation, or sleep disturbances
Signs of allergic reaction (rash, breathing difficulty)
Significant blood pressure changes or heart palpitations
contraindications: Do not exceed 4-week continuous use without medical supervision
monitoring: Start with lowest effective dose (300mcg) to assess individual response
May enhance stimulant effects - reduce other stimulant doses

good: Proper concentration labeling as 0.1% or 1% Semax
Cold chain shipping with cold packs

references.title: A Nootropic Adrenocorticotropin Analog 4-10 Semax: 15 Years Experience in Its Design and Study
references.authors: Ashmarin IP, Nezavibatko VN, Levitskaya NG, et al.
references.journal: Zhurnal Vysshei Nervnoi Deiatelnosti imeni I.P. Pavlova
references.keyFindings: Foundational 15-year review: Semax stimulates operative memory and attention, increases hypoxia resistance, and improves brain circulation for 20-24 hours after intranasal doses of 0.015-0.050 mg/kg.

references.title: The Efficacy of Semax in the Treatment of Patients at Different Stages of Ischemic Stroke
references.authors: Gusev EI, Barskov IV, et al.
references.journal: Zhurnal Nevrologii i Psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
references.keyFindings: 110 patients after ischemic stroke; Semax (6000 mcg/day intranasal) increased BDNF plasma levels, sped functional recovery, and improved motor performance during rehabilitation.

references.title: Effects of Semax on the Default Mode Network of the Brain
references.authors: Dolgorukova AM, Klyushnik TP, Gusev EI, et al.
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: 24 healthy volunteers (43.9 +/- 9.5 years); resting-state fMRI showed increased default mode network volume in medial frontal cortex after intranasal 1% Semax vs placebo.

references.title: The Peptide Semax Affects the Expression of Genes Related to the Immune and Vascular Systems in Rat Brain Focal Ischemia: Genome-Wide Transcriptional Analysis
references.authors: Dergunova LV, Limborska SA, et al.
references.keyFindings: Genome-wide study of ischemized rat brain cortex: Semax modulated expression of 24 vascular genes at 3h and enhanced immune-response gene expression at 24h post-pMCAO, including immunoglobulin and chemokine groups.

references.title: Semax, an Analog of ACTH(4-10) with Cognitive Effects, Regulates BDNF and trkB Expression in the Rat Hippocampus
references.authors: Dolotov OV, Karpenko EA, Inozemtseva LS, et al.
references.keyFindings: Single application at 50 mcg/kg produced 1.4-fold increase in BDNF protein and 1.6-fold increase in trkB tyrosine phosphorylation in rat hippocampus.

latestResearch.title: The Potential of the Peptide Drug Semax and Its Derivative for Correcting Pathological Impairments in the Animal Model of Alzheimer's Disease
latestResearch.summary: Semax and its derivative improved cognitive functions in transgenic Alzheimer's mice; amyloid plaque count decreased 2.8-fold in the Semax group versus untreated controls.

latestResearch.title: Semax, a Synthetic Regulatory Peptide, Affects Copper-Induced Abeta Aggregation and Amyloid Formation in Artificial Membrane Models
latestResearch.summary: Semax prevents formation of Abeta:Cu2+ complexes and inhibits amyloid fiber formation, demonstrating anti-aggregating and neuroprotective properties relevant to Alzheimer's disease.

latestResearch.title: Semax Peptide Targets the Mu Opioid Receptor Gene Oprm1 to Promote Functional Recovery After Spinal Cord Injury
latestResearch.summary: Semax improved spinal cord injury functional recovery and inhibited pyroptosis in mice by targeting mu-opioid receptors and regulating the USP18 deubiquitination pathway.

latestResearch.title: Semax, a Copper Chelator Peptide, Decreases Cu(II)-Catalyzed ROS Production and Cytotoxicity of Abeta
latestResearch.summary: Semax extracts Cu(II) from Cu(II)-amyloid-beta species and decreases associated ROS production, showing cytoprotective properties against copper-mediated oxidative stress.

quickStats.typicalDose: 300-600mcg per dose (up to 1000mcg for intensive use)
